source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Referral bonus: If you know someone exceptional feel free to connect us via LinkedIn (Dennis Agidigbi), if we end up hiring them for a full-time role, you'll receive 10,000€.



🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional engineers to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Founding Engineer to help us define and build the next generation of AI-powered logistics systems.



🧠 What You'll Do

You will help turn vision into product and bring our first AI agents into the hands of real users. This is a high-ownership role, working closely with the founding team and customers to build something truly new. You'll operate across the full stack—talking to customers one hour and debugging an agent loop the next—making the foundational technical decisions that define what the product becomes.

• Talk directly to customers and translate their needs into product direction and system architecture

• Build full-stack features that combine intuitive interfaces with intelligent backend logic, including LLM orchestration, agent workflows, and structured data pipelines

• Push the limits of what LLMs and agents can do in production, working through the hard problems of reliability, latency, and evaluation

• Scale from demo-quality code to robust, production-ready systems, making deliberate tradeoffs between speed and durability as the product matures

• Help shape the technical vision and product strategy of the company



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have experience taking full ownership of technical projects, from architecture through to delivery and iteration

• Are pragmatic and fast in the early days, but know when to raise the bar for scale and quality

• Love writing and shipping code, and are not afraid to throw it away when the problem shifts

• Enjoy speaking with users and iterating quickly based on feedback, treating customer insight as a core input to engineering decisions

• Want to build something ambitious and impactful, from the very beginning



🛠 What You Bring

• Strong skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and React, with an instinct for patterns that move fast without creating debt—clean service boundaries, event-driven architectures, thin abstractions that are easy to replace

• Experience with distributed systems and serverless infrastructure that handle concurrency, failure, and scale

• Hands-on MongoDB experience, including schema design that balances query performance with flexibility

• Familiarity with observability tooling, ideally Datadog—tracing across async workflows, using metrics to debug production issues, not just monitor them

• Experience with modern AI tooling: LLMs, vector stores, embedding pipelines, prompt engineering

• Practical understanding of LLM-powered features in production—eval frameworks, structured output parsing, context window management, error recovery

• A track record of shipping products that create real value

• Curiosity for how industries work and a drive to improve them through software

• High-agency mindset and the ability to operate independently in a fast-changing environment



📍 Location & Logistics

• In-person Monday to Wednesday in our Berlin office (travel within Europe expected)

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: As soon as possible

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Referral bonus: If you know someone exceptional feel free to connect us via LinkedIn (Dennis Agidigbi), if we end up hiring them for a full-time role, you'll receive 10,000€.



🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional engineers to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Forward Deployed Engineer https://a16z.com/services-led-growth/ to help us build with and for customers right in the field. Probably the hottest role in startups right now.



🧠 What You'll Do

You'll be the bridge between customer pain and product reality. You will:

• Sit with logistics teams, understand workflows, and turn chaos into clear system design

• Build fast, AI-powered tools across the stack (Node.js, TypeScript)

• Integrate with enterprise ecosystems (ERP, TMS, SFTP, event queues) and build solid pipelines for messy data

• Shape product direction and tech decisions with the founding team

• Travel with us to key accounts in Europe and Asia



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Thrive in early-stage environments and take full ownership of outcomes

• Write clean, fast code and love getting things into production

• Communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

• Are willing to work hard, to create something new and groundbreaking

• Enjoy traveling and working directly with customers



🛠 What You Bring

• Fluency in TypeScript/Node.js; frontend React is a plus

• Experience with APIs, external integrations, and enterprise data systems

• Ability to design and scale backend infrastructure

• Familiarity with cloud infra (Docker, Terraform, GCP or AWS)

• Bonus: experience working with GenAI/LLM architectures (RAG, evals, orchestration)



📍 Location & Logistics

• Berlin office preferred, but open for hybrid setups

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: As soon as possible



💼 What We Offer

• Top-of-market compensation and meaningful early equity

• Direct exposure to customers and decision-making

• A small team of excellent engineers and operators with high autonomy

• A mission that matters: building intelligent systems to power the future of global trade



🚀 Why Now

We’re not a demo company. We’re in production with real customers, in one of the world’s most critical and complex industries. Demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. This is your chance to join extremely early, shape the company, and build what will become the core infrastructure for global logistics automation.

We envision a world where every shipment is proactively managed by intelligent agents. Where operators have superhuman capabilities at their fingertips and trade never stalls.



🇪🇺 Building from Europe

What drives us personally is the belief that Europe’s long-term prosperity and sovereignty depend on a strong economic engine. We need more AI product-first European global champions on the world stage.

Our mission is to build one of the biggest automation companies in the world - from Europe. While our HQ will be in Europe, we will relentlessly benchmark ourselves with the best globally and sell internationally from the beginning.

source_jobs.title: Founding Designer
source_jobs.description:
🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what's still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it's still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Founding Designer to help craft the agent-native interfaces powering the future of global logistic systems.



🧠 What You'll Do

This is a high-ownership role. You will take full responsibility for our product’s UX and UI. You will work side by side with customers, engineers, and the founding team to shape how operators collaborate with intelligent systems.

• Lead the full design process from first insight to final UI

• Create intuitive and powerful interfaces for AI-driven workflows

• Build and maintain a simple, effective design system

• Work directly with freight forwarders to deeply understand their needs

• Run hands-on research and testing to validate your ideas quickly



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have worked as a product designer in an early-stage or fast-paced team

• Care deeply about solving real user problems and not just making things pretty

• Love speaking to users and working with engineers to get things shipped

• Have experience with complex workflows, ideally in B2B or operations

• Are excited about AI and want to define new ways of working with intelligent systems



🛠 What You Bring

• A strong portfolio showing thoughtful UX work and clean product UIs

• Confidence using tools like Figma to move fast and build systems

• Curiosity for how complex industries actually work

• Ability to turn messy workflows into elegant digital experiences

• Bonus: understanding of logistics, experience designing AI-powered tools or coding skills



📍 Location & Logistics

• Berlin office preferred, but open for hybrid setups

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: Flexible



💼 What We Offer

• Top-of-market compensation and meaningful early equity

• A role with real ownership and the chance to define a product from scratch

• A close-knit team that values action, clarity, and customer impact

• Access to real customers and the full design-to-deployment loop

• A shot at designing a product that changes how the world’s goods move



🚀 Why Now

AI tools are powerful, but the product layer is missing. The current operating systems of our users are >15 years old and looking like Windows XP. We're not a demo company — we're in production with real customers, and demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. This is your chance to define how humans work with machines in one of the most important industries in the world.

We envision a world where every shipment is proactively managed by intelligent agents. Where operators have superhuman capabilities at their fingertips and trade never stalls.



🇪🇺 Building from Europe

What drives us personally is the belief that Europe’s long-term prosperity and sovereignty depend on a strong economic engine. We need more AI product-first European global champions on the world stage.

Our mission is to build one of the biggest automation companies in the world - from Europe. While our HQ will be in Europe, we will relentlessly benchmark ourselves with the best globally and sell internationally from the beginning.

source_jobs.title: Founders Associate Intern
source_jobs.description:
🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Founder's Associate Intern helping us grow and bring Zauber to the next stage.



🧠 What You'll Do

You'll be a core part of a small and fast-moving team. This is a hands-on, high-ownership role where you will help us scale both product and company operations while learning directly from experienced operators and founders along the way.

• Build and manage internal systems like our website and tooling

• Lead recruiting efforts and support onboarding of new team members

• Support go-to-market initiatives and help bring our agents to customers

• Identify and secure grant and non-dilutive funding opportunities

• Tackle whatever challenge moves the company forward



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Enjoy solving a wide range of problems in dynamic environments

• Are curious about startups, AI, and how companies are built

• Take full ownership and pride in execution

• Are able to switch between big-picture thinking and detailed execution

• Aspire to build something meaningful and potentially start your own company



🛠 What You Bring

• Experience in generalist roles, ideally in early-stage or high-agency environments

• A somewhat technical background or strong intuition for AI and software

• Strong communication skills in both German and English

• High integrity, strong work ethic, and a sense of urgency



📍 Location & Logistics

• Berlin office preferred

• Start: Q4 2026

• Compensation: 2,400€ per month



💼 What We Offer

• A role with real responsibility from day one

• A close-knit team that values action, clarity, and customer impact

• Access to experienced operators and strong mentorship

• The chance to shape one of Europe’s most ambitious AI startups



🚀 Why Now

We’re not a demo company. We’re in production with real customers, in one of the world’s most critical and complex industries. Demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. Logistics remains largely untouched by intelligent automation — legacy systems, manual processes, and fragmented workflows make it one of the most fertile grounds for AI transformation.

This is your opportunity to learn company building by doing. You will not be a passive observer but an active driver of what gets built and how we grow.

We envision a world where every shipment is proactively managed by intelligent agents. Where operators have superhuman capabilities at their fingertips and trade never stalls.



🇪🇺 Building from Europe

What drives us personally is the belief that Europe’s long-term prosperity and sovereignty depend on a strong economic engine. We need more AI product-first European global champions on the world stage.

Our mission is to build one of the biggest automation companies in the world - from Europe. While our HQ will be in Europe, we will relentlessly benchmark ourselves with the best globally and sell internationally from the beginning.

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
Referral bonus: If you know someone exceptional feel free to connect us via LinkedIn (Dennis Agidigbi), if we end up hiring them for a full-time role, you'll receive 10,000€.



🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Founding Product Lead to help us define and build the next generation of AI-powered logistics systems.



🧠 What You'll Do

You will own our product strategy and execution entirely, defining the roadmap for our AI agent platform from the ground up. This is a high-ownership role where you'll define what we build and how we build it, translating messy operational challenges into elegant agent architectures that work in production. Every decision you make in these early stages shapes how logistics companies will operate for the next decade.

• Define the product vision and roadmap for Zauber's AI agent platform, balancing individual customer needs with technical feasibility, scalability and business impact

• Work directly with enterprise customers to understand their workflows and pain points, then translate those into clear product requirements and agent architectures

• Partner closely with engineering to design system integrations and user experiences that handle the reality of freight forwarding operations

• Own the full product lifecycle from discovery to launch, including use case and feature prioritization, success metrics, and iteration based on production data



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have 5+ years of product management experience building technical products, ideally in AI, automation, or enterprise software

• Are comfortable with ambiguity and can make clear decisions with incomplete information

• Have strong technical intuition and can work effectively with engineers without needing to write production code yourself



🛠 What You Bring

• Proven track record shipping products that customers actually use and get value from

• Deep understanding of how agentic LLM products work, including their capabilities and limitations in production

• Strong ability to translate ambiguous customer problems into clear product requirements and technical specifications abstracting custom requirements into scalable solutions

• Willingness to become a domain expert and learn to immerse yourself in the day-to-day of our customers and users

• Fluency in English required, German is a strong plus



📍 Location & Logistics

• In-person Monday to Wednesday in our Berlin office (travel within Europe expected)

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: As soon as possible



💼 What We Offer

• Top-of-market compensation and meaningful early equity

• Direct access to real users and fast feedback loops

• A highly experienced team with full trust and autonomy

• The opportunity to shape the foundations of a company and product from day one

• A mission that matters: building intelligent systems to power the future of global trade



🚀 Why Now

We’re not a demo company. We’re in production with real customers, in one of the world’s most critical and complex industries. Demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. This is your chance to join extremely early, shape the company, and build what will become the core infrastructure for global

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Enterprise Sales Manager
source_jobs.description:
🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional (Senior) Enterprise Sales Manager to help us define and build the next generation of AI-powered logistics systems



🧠 What You'll Do

You will own the full sales cycle with enterprise freight forwarders, from first conversation to signed contract. This is a high-ownership role where you'll shape how Zauber sells into one of the world's largest industries. The deals you close in these early stages directly shape the company's trajectory and growth.

• Own the entire enterprise and top mid-market sales process with global freight forwarders (€0.5-30bn revenue, C-level buyers), converting active pilots into multi-year contracts

• Build relationships with senior decision-makers at globally leading forwarders and regional champions

• Work directly with the Commercial Lead and Founder to refine messaging, positioning, and commercial terms

• Develop and iterate repeatable sales playbooks as we scale from founder-led to team-led Sales

• Drive pricing strategy, contract negotiation, and deal structure while partnering with product and engineering to translate customer feedback into roadmap priorities

• Share, synthesize and discuss customer insights and feedbacks with Zauber team



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have at least 3-7 years of enterprise sales experience, ideally selling technology and/or software products into traditional industries

• Know how to navigate enterprise organizations with long sales cycles and multiple stakeholders

• Are comfortable operating in ambiguity, constant change and building the playbook while executing it

• Thrive in founder-led environments and can switch between strategic thinking and hands-on execution

• Enjoy fast-paced, excellence, and driven environments



🛠 What You Bring

• Proven track record closing six- and seven-figure enterprise deals with Fortune 500 or equivalent companies

• Experience selling SaaS, AI, or automation products into operations-heavy or legacy industries

• Ability to run discovery processes, build business cases, and quantify ROI in complex operational environments

• Ability to build strong personal relationships and trust among various stakeholders and groups

• Excellent written and verbal communication in English and German (additional European languages a plus)

• Willingness to travel regularly across Europe and occasionally to Asia/North America



📍 Location & Logistics

• In-person at least a few days a week in our Berlin and/or Hamburg office

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: As soon as possible



💼 What We Offer

• Top-of-market compensation and meaningful early equity

• A highly experienced team with full trust and autonomy

• The opportunity to shape the GTM foundations of a company and product from day one

• A mission that matters: building intelligent systems to power the future of global trade



🚀 Why Now

We’re not a demo company. We’re in production with real customers, in one of the world’s most critical and complex industries. Demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. This is your chance to join extremely early, shape the company, and build what will become the core infrastructure for global logistic

source_jobs.title: Agent Deployment Manager
source_jobs.description:
🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Agent Deployment Manager to help us define and build the next generation of AI-powered logistics systems.



🧠 What You'll Do

You will own the complete deployment lifecycle for Zauber's AI agents with global freight forwarders, turning signed contracts into production systems that deliver measurable business value. This is a high-ownership role where you'll define how we implement, integrate, and scale our AI agents across complex enterprise environments. Every implementation you lead in these early stages builds the foundation for how we onboard hundreds of customers in the future.

• Own end-to-end deployment for enterprise customers, from kickoff through go-live and hypercare to large scale roll-out and use-case expansion

• Work directly with customer stakeholders across operations, IT, and business teams to map workflows, configure agents, and drive adoption

• Partner closely with engineering/product to troubleshoot technical issues, coordinate system integrations, and feed customer insights back to product

• Contribute to improving deployment playbooks, escalation processes, and operational readiness over time, incl. creating repeatable processes, templates, and best practices that enable the team to scale while compressing deployment cycles and time-to-value

• Drive change management and operator training, turning skeptical users into AI champions who trust and rely on the agents



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have 2+ years of experience in implementation, deployment, or technical project management, ideally with enterprise software or AI products in a B2B environment

• Know how to navigate complex organizations, build trust with diverse stakeholders, and drive consensus across teams

• Have a bias for action and getting things done, even when the path forward is unclear

• Experience with Agents/LLMs or writing software yourself is a big plus, as well as any working experience in the logistics/freight forwarding industry



🛠 What You Bring

• Strong understanding of how enterprise software works, including APIs, databases, system architectures and integrations

• Excellent project management skills, with the ability to plan, coordinate, and deliver on time across multiple stakeholders

• Outstanding communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences

• Fluency in English required, German is a strong plus



📍 Location & Logistics

• Berlin office preferred, but open for hybrid setups

• Annual offsite (last year we took the team for 4 weeks to San Francisco)

• Start: As soon as possible



💼 What We Offer

• Top-of-market compensation and meaningful early equity

• Direct access to real users and fast feedback loops

• A highly experienced team with full trust and autonomy

• The opportunity to shape the foundations of a company and product from day one

• A mission that matters: building intelligent systems to power the future of global trade

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Referral bonus: If you know someone exceptional feel free to connect us via LinkedIn (Dennis Agidigbi), if we end up hiring them for a full-time role, you'll receive 10,000€.



🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what’s still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it’s still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional engineers to join early

Now we are looking for an exceptional Platform Engineer to help us define and build the next generation of AI-powered logistics systems.



🧠 What You'll Do

You will build the infrastructure that our AI agents run on. We've made pragmatic early tooling choices for speed, and as we scale, some will hold and some won't. We need someone who understands the distributed systems principles underneath modern tooling, and can make the right call on when to keep things simple and when to reach for something heavier. This is a foundational role, and you'll design the systems the rest of the team builds on.

• Build and operate the runtime infrastructure that powers our AI agents in production, the systems that customers depend on

• Design for concurrency and failure, including queuing, retries, backpressure, and idempotency, so agent workflows stay reliable under real-world load

• Own production reliability: latency budgets, error budgets, capacity planning, and incident response

• Instrument the stack with tracing, structured logging, and alerting so problems surface before users notice them

• Own the deployment pipeline end-to-end: CI/CD, preview and staging environments, and the path from local dev to production

• Build internal tooling and abstractions that let the product team ship faster without worrying about infrastructure



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have experience owning infrastructure end-to-end at a startup, where you picked the tools and lived with the consequences

• Understand distributed systems deeply enough to know what managed platforms are doing under the hood, and where their abstractions will break

• Care about developer experience: deployment pipelines, local dev setups, error messages, and the small things that compound into engineering velocity

• Prefer to automate yourself out of toil rather than accept it

• Want to lay the foundation at a company where infrastructure decisions directly shape the product



🛠 What You Bring

• Strong understanding of distributed systems: concurrency, eventual consistency, event-driven architectures, and failure modes. You have the mental models even if you've implemented them with modern serverless tools rather than raw AWS primitives

• Experience with CI/CD design: multi-environment pipelines, automated testing gates, preview deployments, infrastructure-as-code

• Hands-on experience with platforms like Vercel, and an honest sense of where they scale and where they don't

• Familiarity with workflow orchestration tools like Inngest, Temporal, or Step Functions, and the queueing and retry semantics underneath them

• MongoDB experience: schema design tradeoffs, indexing strategy, and performance tuning as data and query patterns evolve

• Strong observability practice, ideally Datadog, including distributed tracing, custom metrics, and SLO-based alerting

• Enough AWS fluency (ECS, Lambda, SQS, IAM) to know what you'd reach for when the managed tools hit their limits

• High-agency mindset and comfort operating independently in a fast-changi

source_jobs.title: Founders Associate
source_jobs.description:
🌍 About Zauber

After co-founding and scaling Forto https://forto.com to over $600M in funding, I took a step back to reflect on what's still broken in global logistics. The answer was clear: it's still a human-first, spreadsheet-heavy industry, full of repetitive processes and broken system interoperability.

With the rise of AI agents, we now have the tools to rethink that from the ground up. Zauber builds autonomous agents for the global logistics industry, AI workers that handle operational tasks, talk to systems and humans alike, and get better every day.

🚢 We're already live with global logistics leaders in Europe and Asia

💰 Backed by P9 - one of Europe's top early-stage funds

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early

We are now looking for an exceptional Founders Associate to help us scale Zauber into its next phase. This is a full-time role with significant responsibility, designed for someone who wants to operate at the center of a fast-growing company and grow into a senior operating or founding role over time.



🧠 What You'll Do

You'll be a core part of a small and fast-moving team, working directly with the founders on the highest-leverage problems across the company. This is a hands-on, high-ownership role where you will help us scale both product and company operations, while taking end-to-end responsibility for critical workstreams.

• Own and run cross-functional projects across finance, operations, compliance, and people

• Build and manage internal systems, tooling, and automations that let the company scale

• Lead recruiting efforts end-to-end and support onboarding of new team members

• Support go-to-market initiatives and help bring our agents to enterprise customers

• Step into whatever challenge moves the company forward, from board prep to vendor management



👤 You Might Be a Fit If You...

• Have already proven yourself in a generalist or operating role and want more responsibility

• Enjoy solving a wide range of problems in dynamic, ambiguous environments

• Take full ownership and pride in execution, including the unglamorous details

• Are able to switch between big-picture thinking and detailed execution within the same day

• Are curious about startups, AI, and how category-defining companies get built

• Aspire to build something meaningful and potentially start your own company later



🛠 What You Bring

• 2+ years of experience in generalist, operating, consulting, or founding roles, ideally in early-stage or high-agency environments

• A technical background or strong intuition for AI, software, and automation, with the ability to build internal tooling yourself

• Strong communication skills in both German and English

• High integrity, strong work ethic, and a sense of urgency

• Comfort owning outcomes end-to-end without needing close supervision



📍 Location & Logistics

• Berlin office, in person preferred

• Start: as soon as possible

• Full-time, permanent role

• Competitive salary plus meaningful equity participation



💼 What We Offer

• A role with real responsibility and end-to-end ownership from day one

• Meaningful equity in one of Europe's most ambitious AI companies

• A close-knit team that values action, clarity, and customer impact

• Direct access to experienced operators and founders, with a clear path to grow into senior operating or founding responsibility

• The chance to shape one of Europe's most ambitious AI startups during its formative years



🚀 Why Now

We're not a demo company. We're in production with real customers, in one of the world's most critical and complex industries. Demand is not our bottleneck, execution is. Logistics remains largely untouched by intelligent automation. Legacy systems, manual processes, and fragmented workflows make it one of the most fertile grounds for AI transformation.

This is your opportunity to learn company building by doing, at a level of responsibility that is hard to find anywhere else. You w

notes: Early-stage startup with strong engineering focus, building autonomous agents for global logistics.
